title: Release Gates, Capacity, Compliance, Migration, Rollback, and Defense · Transfer Build
summary: An independent site owner provides a different origin, URL space, markup fault, indexing policy, accessibility need, resource budget, dynamic boundary, and failure condition and transfers release gates, capacity, compliance, migration, rollback, and defense into a changed production crawler evidence and recovery defense. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of production crawler evidence and recovery defense with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress thr…
objectives: Reconcile authorization, scope, robots, HTTP, URL, parse, extraction, crawl graph, metadata, accessibility, output, and changed-case evidence.
Measure pages, resources, bytes, parse time, memory, queue, concurrency, browser load, output, cancellation, and recovery at representative scale.
Obtain accountable owner, privacy, security, accessibility, data, legal-review, operations, and target-site approvals with expiry and revocation paths.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Correctness Reconciliation
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab site-quality team handles case 9808 during an authorized nonprofit content audit. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend end-to-end correctness, capacity, accountable approvals, migration, owner pause, rollback, recovery, and residual risk; the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure; then change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Audit the proposed evidence packet · Lab Production Defense
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab site-quality team handles case 9094 during an authorized nonprofit content audit. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend end-to-end correctness, capacity, accountable approvals, migration, owner pause, rollback, recovery, and residual risk; no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted; then replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend the system under an unfamiliar site, block, malformed page, privacy request, overload, partial failure, browser fault, and owner revocation and state residual risk.

Inspect the Production Defense trace for this lab case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Audit the proposed evidence packet · Lab Production Defense.

The Production Defense record says production crawler evidence and recovery defense looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the audit the proposed evidence packet evidence boundary for Production Defense.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Production Defense
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-production-defense-oniy24" that uses case 2456 to defend end-to-end correctness, capacity, accountable approvals, migration, owner pause, rollback, recovery, and residual risk. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Production Defense.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Production Defense into observable performance evidence. For Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Production Defense, keep Production Defense connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-production-defense-oniy24
# Competency: scraper-production-defense.
# Case 2456: a keyboard and screen-reader report review.
# Operating constraint: automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance.
# Changed case: introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it.
def evidence_scraper_production_defense_oniy24(release_gates: int = 16, passed_gates: int = 16, rehearsed_failures: int = 8, open_critical_findings: int = 0, owner_pause_seconds: int = 20) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_oniy24 = "oniy24-42yino-2456"
    assert evidence_variant_oniy24
    evidence_axes_oniy24 = (
        "authorizationoniy24",
        "url2456",
        "response42yino",
        "treeoniy242456",
        "frontier2456oniy24",
        "report42yino2456",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_oniy24)) == 6
    if release_gates < 12 or passed_gates < 0:
        return False, passed_gates, "release-depth"
    if passed_gates != release_gates:
        return False, passed_gates, "failed-release-gate"
    if rehearsed_failures < 6 or open_critical_findings > 0:
        return False, open_critical_findings, "recovery-gap"
    if owner_pause_seconds < 0 or owner_pause_seconds > 60:
        return False, owner_pause_seconds, "owner-stop-budget"
    assert passed_gates == release_gates and open_critical_findings == 0
    return True, passed_gates, "defensible-crawler-production"
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Production Defense structural check is only one gate. production crawler evidence and recovery defense must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.
